attacking_style: Opportunistic attacker who strikes from breakaways or in final 2-3km of climbs, preferring to exploit tactical situations rather than pure power attacks. Most effective when launching from reduced groups rather than splitting large pelotons.
team_tactics: Limited evidence of strong team support; mechanical incident showed less protection than expected for GC contender. Appears to rely on individual positioning and tactical awareness rather than sustained team tempo control.
terrain_preference: Performs best on medium-gradient climbs where positioning and tactical timing matter more than pure watts. Strong in reduced sprint finishes from small groups. Struggles on steepest mountain finishes and in extended multi-day high-mountain efforts.
vulnerability: Fades badly in consecutive high-mountain stages, particularly when distanced before final climbs begin. Week 1 leadership positions prove unsustainable. Lacks attacking capacity when marked closely in final GC battles.
key_patterns: Excels in small-group sprint finishes, winning stages through superior positioning and timing (3 observations)
Week 1 GC gains prove unsustainable; consistently loses time in final mountain stages (4 observations)
Effective from breakaways but defensively weak when marked by pure climbers (3 observations)
Shows tactical sharpness in varied-pace situations but lacks sustained high-mountain power (4 observations)
Collects bonus seconds effectively when opportunities arise, closing small GC gaps (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 Tour showed clear decline from 2022 Giro-winning form. Lost yellow jersey mid-race, distanced before final climbs, and demonstrated no attacking capacity in GC defense. Podium lost by 1 second after being dropped multiple times.
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 20
